goRule: A preparation artifact is only ready when the required handoff blocks exist, every required block carries task-specific rewritten content with provenance, the active CTO context surfaces are covered or deliberately excluded, and no blocking missing evidence remains.

phases.goal: Write the sharpest possible questions for the context store before any broad retrieval so the right CTO memory surfaces can be activated.
Rewrite the active context package block by block from evidence instead of copying raw snippets or inventing prompt text.
Stress-test the draft context package against relevance, freshness, provenance, memory-surface coverage, noise risk, and missing-evidence risk before execution.

blocks.title: Goal And Authority
blocks.goal: State the exact task objective, owner authority, and why this step matters now as execution metadata, not as a substitute for context.

blocks.title: Definition Of Done
blocks.goal: Spell out what would count as real completion for the active task instead of vague progress.

blocks.title: Verified World State
blocks.goal: Summarize only the currently verified facts, paths, runtime state, and artifacts relevant to the next step.

blocks.title: Relevant Artifacts
blocks.goal: Name the concrete files, contracts, skills, checkpoints, or commands the next step must touch.

blocks.title: Constraints And Policies
blocks.goal: Capture only the rules, boundaries, and approvals that materially constrain the next action.

blocks.title: Open Questions
blocks.goal: List the still unresolved questions that can change the next action.

blocks.title: Next Action Only
blocks.goal: Describe the single highest-value next direct step and what evidence it should produce.

blocks.title: Risks And Failure Modes
blocks.goal: Name the immediate ways the next step can go wrong, especially stale or irrelevant context.

blocks.title: Excluded Context
blocks.goal: Explicitly name the tempting but irrelevant context that must stay out of the active prompt.

surfaces.title: System Identity
surfaces.goal: Anchor the affected system, repo, product, environment, and task family so the next run knows which technical world it is operating in.
activationHints: repo root, product, branch, deployment target
model/runtime family when it materially changes the work

surfaces.title: Live Operational State
surfaces.goal: Capture the currently true runtime state, active sessions, processes, failures, and resource conditions that can change the next step.
activationHints: active exec sessions, running services, recent errors
fresh runtime evidence beats old assumptions

surfaces.title: Architecture Surface
surfaces.goal: Name only the relevant components, boundaries, interfaces, and dependency paths that shape the next decision or action.

surfaces.title: Artifact Surface
surfaces.goal: Bring in the concrete files, tables, contracts, checkpoints, logs, and commands that the next run is likely to inspect or modify.
activationHints: paths and artifact refs should be concrete
prefer must-touch artifacts over broad repo trivia

surfaces.title: Change Lineage
surfaces.goal: Summarize the recent change path, the latest observed effect, and any still-open follow-up created by those changes.

surfaces.title: Decision Lineage
surfaces.goal: Preserve the relevant rationale, tradeoffs, and rejected options so the next run does not reopen settled decisions blindly.

surfaces.title: Failure And Learning Memory
surfaces.goal: Activate prior failures, durable learnings, and known traps that should change the next move in a concrete way.
activationHints: known traps and repeated failure patterns

surfaces.title: Resource Envelope
surfaces.goal: Make hard limits visible: permissions, disk, memory, GPU, tool availability, and other resource boundaries that can block the next run.

surfaces.title: Stakeholder Surface
surfaces.goal: Include only the people, ownership, approval, and communication context that materially constrain the technical decision.
activationHints: approval dependency when it changes the next action

surfaces.title: Uncertainty And Conflict Surface
surfaces.goal: Mark stale evidence, unresolved conflicts, uncertain claims, and freshness risks instead of silently flattening them away.

surfaces.title: Trigger Vocabulary
surfaces.goal: Carry the aliases, entity names, model names, file patterns, and project-specific keywords that reactivate the right memory region.

surfaces.title: Excluded Noise
surfaces.goal: Explicitly exclude tempting but irrelevant history, adjacent systems, and broad trivia so the next run does not drown in ballast.
activationHints: broad history that should stay out

negativeSignals.title: System Scope Ambiguous
negativeSignals.criterion: The active system, repo, product, or environment is anchored clearly enough that the next run cannot drift into the wrong technical world.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context leaves the system boundary vague or mixes multiple possible scopes.

negativeSignals.title: Wrong System Anchor
negativeSignals.criterion: The anchor points to the correct repo, product, environment, or task family for the pending run.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context is anchored to the wrong system, repo, product, or environment.

negativeSignals.title: Environment Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The environment or deployment target is present when it can change the next step materially.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The relevant environment anchor is missing even though it matters for the next move.

negativeSignals.title: Live State Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The package contains the runtime state, session state, or failure state that the next run actually depends on.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: Current operational state is missing even though the next run depends on it.

negativeSignals.title: Stale Runtime State
negativeSignals.criterion: Runtime claims are fresh enough that the next run can trust them.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context relies on runtime evidence that is likely stale for the pending run.

negativeSignals.title: Session Continuity Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: An existing session or process lineage is included when it would avoid reconstructing shell or runtime state from scratch.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: Relevant session continuity is omitted, so the next run may recreate already-running state blindly.

negativeSignals.title: Component Boundary Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The relevant component or interface boundary is visible enough to scope the next action.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context names work to do but leaves the important component or interface boundary unclear.

negativeSignals.title: Dependency Chain Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The dependency path that can make the next action fail is present when it matters.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context omits the relevant upstream or downstream dependency path.

negativeSignals.title: Architecture Noise
negativeSignals.criterion: Architecture references stay scoped to the components that shape the next step.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The architecture surface includes broad or decorative system detail that does not change the next move.

negativeSignals.title: Critical Artifact Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Critical files, contracts, checkpoints, commands, or logs are present when the next run will need them.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A must-touch artifact is missing from the context package.

negativeSignals.title: Artifact Refs Thin
negativeSignals.criterion: Artifact references are concrete enough to be actionable.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: Artifact references stay too vague to drive the next run directly.

negativeSignals.title: Artifact Noise Dominates
negativeSignals.criterion: The artifact surface favors must-touch artifacts over broad repo trivia.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: Too much low-value artifact detail is present relative to what the next run will actually touch.

negativeSignals.title: Recent Change Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Recent changes are included when they materially shape the pending run.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: Relevant recent changes are omitted, so the next run loses the short-term storyline.

negativeSignals.title: Change Effect Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The latest observed effect of a relevant change is visible when it changes the next step.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The change is mentioned, but its effect or current status is not.

negativeSignals.title: Lineage Cut
negativeSignals.criterion: The package preserves enough continuity that the next run knows how the current state emerged.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context jumps into the middle of the situation without enough change lineage.

negativeSignals.title: Decision Rationale Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Relevant earlier decisions carry the reason why they were made.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A relevant prior decision is visible only as an outcome, not as a rationale.

negativeSignals.title: Tradeoff Hidden
negativeSignals.criterion: The tradeoff behind a still-binding decision is included when it affects the next step.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package hides the tradeoff, so the next run may reopen or misread a settled decision.

negativeSignals.title: Rejected Option Lost
negativeSignals.criterion: A previously rejected option is mentioned when forgetting it would recreate a known detour.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The context forgets a rejected option that still matters as a boundary.

negativeSignals.title: Prior Failure Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The package activates prior failures that should change the next move concretely.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A relevant prior failure is absent even though it should change the next step.

negativeSignals.title: Learning Not Activated
negativeSignals.criterion: Durable learnings are activated when they materially reduce risk or wasted work.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A relevant durable learning is visible in memory but not activated in the package.

negativeSignals.title: Known Trap Repeated
negativeSignals.criterion: Known traps are marked early enough that the next run can avoid them.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package would let the next run repeat a known trap without warning.

negativeSignals.title: Hard Limit Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Hard limits and approval boundaries are present whenever they can block the next run.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A blocking limit or approval boundary is missing from context.

negativeSignals.title: Tool Boundary Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Tool availability and missing-tool boundaries are visible when they change the next step.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package leaves tool availability or tool limits implicit when they matter.

negativeSignals.title: Capacity State Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Current capacity state is present when storage, memory, GPU, or similar constraints are decision-relevant.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The relevant host capacity state is missing from context.

negativeSignals.title: Decision Owner Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: The package identifies the owner or decider when approval or intent can change the next move.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A relevant owner or decision boundary is missing.

negativeSignals.title: Approval Dependency Hidden
negativeSignals.criterion: Approval dependencies are visible when they can block or redirect the next action.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package hides a human approval dependency that affects the next run.

negativeSignals.title: Conflict Hidden
negativeSignals.criterion: Conflicting evidence is marked explicitly when the next run could choose the wrong branch otherwise.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package flattens conflicting evidence instead of naming the conflict.

negativeSignals.title: Uncertainty Suppressed
negativeSignals.criterion: Uncertain or weakly verified claims stay marked as uncertain.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package presents uncertain claims as if they were settled facts.

negativeSignals.title: Freshness Risk Ignored
negativeSignals.criterion: Freshness risk is called out when the age of evidence can change the next step materially.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package ignores a freshness risk that could invalidate the next move.

negativeSignals.title: Trigger Terms Thin
negativeSignals.criterion: The package contains enough task-specific keywords, aliases, and entity names to reactivate the right memory region.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The retrieval vocabulary is too thin to trigger the right memory region reliably.

negativeSignals.title: Entity Alias Missing
negativeSignals.criterion: Important aliases or alternate names are present when the system uses them in logs, files, or prior memory.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A relevant alias set is missing, so retrieval and recall may stay shallow.

negativeSignals.title: Retrieval Vocabulary Weak
negativeSignals.criterion: The vocabulary is specific enough to separate the relevant memory region from nearby but irrelevant regions.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The vocabulary is too generic, so it cannot separate the right memory region from distractors.

negativeSignals.title: Noise Dominates
negativeSignals.criterion: The package stays focused enough that the next run sees the relevant context first.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: Ballast or adjacent history dominates the package.

negativeSignals.title: Distractor Not Excluded
negativeSignals.criterion: Tempting but irrelevant context is named explicitly when failing to exclude it would mislead the next run.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: A likely distractor is not excluded even though it competes with the active context.

negativeSignals.title: History Dump Present
negativeSignals.criterion: History is compressed deliberately instead of dumped wholesale into the package.
negativeSignals.reviewSignal: The package contains a broad history dump instead of selective context.

positiveSignals.title: System Scope Explicit
positiveSignals.criterion: The package anchors the correct technical world clearly.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: The active repo, product, environment, and task family are clearly anchored.

positiveSignals.title: Live State Current
positiveSignals.criterion: The package contains fresh live state that changes the next move materially.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: Fresh operational state is present and directly useful.

positiveSignals.title: Relevant Structure Visible
positiveSignals.criterion: The relevant components, boundaries, or dependency path are visible enough to scope the next run correctly.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: The package exposes the relevant technical structure without broad architecture drift.

positiveSignals.title: Artifact Refs Concrete
positiveSignals.criterion: The package gives concrete artifact refs that the next run can touch immediately.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: Artifact refs are concrete, actionable, and appropriately scoped.

positiveSignals.title: Lineage Connected
positiveSignals.criterion: Relevant change and decision lineage are connected enough that the next run understands how the current state emerged.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: Recent changes and decision context are connected into a usable storyline.

positiveSignals.title: Learning Transfer Active
positiveSignals.criterion: Prior failures and durable learnings are activated in a way that changes the next move concretely.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: The package activates relevant lessons and known traps instead of rediscovering them.

positiveSignals.title: Resource Boundaries Clear
positiveSignals.criterion: Hard limits, tool availability, and approval boundaries are visible enough to keep the next run realistic.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: Relevant resource and approval boundaries are explicit.

positiveSignals.title: Uncertainty Honest
positiveSignals.criterion: Conflicts, freshness risk, and uncertainty are marked honestly instead of flattened away.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: The package treats uncertain or conflicting evidence honestly.

positiveSignals.title: Retrieval Triggers Strong
positiveSignals.criterion: The package contains enough aliases, entity names, and task-specific trigger terms to reactivate the right memory region.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: Trigger vocabulary is rich enough to reactivate the right memory region reliably.

positiveSignals.title: Noise Disciplined
positiveSignals.criterion: The package stays selective and explicitly excludes likely distractors.
positiveSignals.reviewSignal: Ballast is actively kept out, and likely distractors are named as exclusions.

assessmentDimensions.title: System Scope And Identity
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether the package anchors the correct technical world: repo, product, environment, and task family.

assessmentDimensions.title: Operational Currency
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether live state, runtime facts, and freshness-sensitive evidence are current enough for the next run.

assessmentDimensions.title: Artifact And Architecture Relevance
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether the package includes the right technical surfaces, artifacts, and dependency paths without broad drift.

assessmentDimensions.title: Lineage And Rationale Memory
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether relevant change history, decisions, and tradeoffs remain connected enough to steer the next run.

assessmentDimensions.title: Failure And Learning Transfer
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether prior failures, durable learnings, and known traps are activated in a way that changes the next move.

assessmentDimensions.title: Resource And Authority Boundaries
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether resource, tool, permission, and approval boundaries are visible enough to keep the next run realistic.

assessmentDimensions.title: Conflict And Uncertainty Handling
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether stale, conflicting, or uncertain evidence is represented honestly instead of flattened into false certainty.

assessmentDimensions.title: Trigger Quality And Noise Control
assessmentDimensions.goal: Judge whether the package uses strong retrieval vocabulary while actively excluding ballast and distractors.

noteFormula: Score S = 2*D1 + 3*D2 + 3*D3 + 2*D4 + 2*D5 + 2*D6 + 2*D7 + 2*D8, where every dimension is graded internally as 0 (clean), 1 (noticeably weak), or 2 (seriously weak).

noteBands.meaning: The context package is sharply targeted, fresh, and highly trustworthy for the next run.
The package is strong and usable, with only minor context weaknesses.
The package is workable but has noticeable gaps or softness that can still distort execution.
The package is weak enough that execution risk is high unless the context is revised.
The package is severely compromised and likely misses or distorts core context.
The package is not credible as an execution context and should not be trusted.

noteGuardrails: A few broad positive signals do not cancel a core negative signal on the wrong system anchor, missing critical artifacts, or stale runtime state.
If `wrong_system_anchor`, `critical_artifact_missing`, or `stale_runtime_state` is pink, the context note must not be better than 5.
If `conflict_hidden` or `hard_limit_missing` is pink, the preparation artifact must not return `go`.
Use many fine-grained negative signals to diagnose CTO context failures, and use fewer broader positive signals only as confirmation.
